modules.name: device
modules.desc:
This chapter is pending supplementation or improvement...

Marked by SuperMonster003 on Oct 22, 2022.

The device module provides information and operations related to the device, such as getting screen dimensions, memory usage, IMEI, adjusting screen brightness, volume, etc.

Some functions in this module (for example, adjusting volume) require the "Modify system settings" permission. If the permission is not granted, a SecurityException will be thrown and the user will be redirected to the permission settings screen.

properties.name: width
properties.desc: Width of the device screen resolution, for example 1080.

properties.name: height
properties.desc: Height of the device screen resolution, for example 1920.

properties.name: buildId
properties.desc:
Either a changelist number, or a label like "M4-rc20".

Build ID or revision identifier.

properties.name: broad
properties.desc:
The name of the underlying board (e.g. "goldfish").

Board model of the device.

properties.name: brand
properties.desc: The consumer-visible brand associated with the product/hardware (e.g. "Xiaomi", "Huawei").

properties.name: device
properties.desc:
The name of the industrial design.

Name of the device in industrial design.

properties.name: model
properties.desc:
The end-user-visible name for the end product.

Device model.

properties.name: product
properties.desc:
The name of the overall product.

Name of the overall product.

properties.name: bootloader
properties.desc:
The system bootloader version number.

Bootloader version of the device.

properties.name: hardware
properties.desc:
The name of the hardware (from the kernel command line or /proc).

Hardware name of the device (from kernel command line or /proc).

properties.name: fingerprint
properties.desc:
A string that uniquely identifies this build. Do not attempt to parse this value.

Unique identifier of the build.

properties.name: serial
properties.desc:
A hardware serial number, if available. Alphanumeric only, case-insensitive.

Hardware serial number.

properties.name: sdkInt
properties.desc:
The user-visible SDK version of the framework; its possible values are defined in Build.VERSION_CODES.

Android API level. For example, Android 4.4 has sdkInt = 19.

properties.name: incremental
properties.desc: The internal value used by the underlying source control to represent this build. E.g., a perforce changelist number or a git hash.

properties.name: release
properties.desc:
The user-visible version string. E.g., "1.0" or "3.4b5".

Android version number. For example "5.0", "7.1.1".

properties.name: baseOS
properties.desc: The base OS build the product is based on.

properties.name: securityPatch
properties.desc:
The user-visible security patch level.

Security patch level.

properties.name: codename
properties.desc:
The current development codename, or the string "REL" if this is a release build.

Development codename (e.g. "REL" for release builds).

methods.name: getIMEI
methods.desc: Returns the device's IMEI.

methods.name: getAndroidId
methods.desc:
Returns the device's Android ID.

Android ID is a 64-bit integer represented as a hexadecimal string. It is randomly generated the first time the device is used and does not change afterward unless the device is factory reset.

methods.name: getMacAddress
methods.desc:
Returns the device's MAC address. This function requires an active WLAN connection to retrieve it; otherwise, it returns null.

Possible future change: In the future, root privileges may be used to retrieve the correct MAC address even without a WLAN connection. Therefore, do not rely on this function to determine WLAN connectivity.

methods.name: getBrightness
methods.desc: Returns the current (manual) brightness. Range is 0~255.

methods.name: getBrightnessMode
methods.desc: Returns the current brightness mode: 0 for manual brightness, 1 for automatic brightness.

methods.name: setBrightness
methods.desc:
Sets the current manual brightness. If the current mode is automatic brightness, this function will not affect the screen brightness.

This function requires the "Modify system settings" permission. If the permission is not granted, a SecurityException will be thrown and the user will be redirected to the permission settings screen.

methods.name: setBrightnessMode

params.textRaw: `mode` {number} Brightness mode: 0 for manual, 1 for automatic
params.name: mode
params.desc: Brightness mode: 0 for manual, 1 for automatic

methods.desc:
Sets the current brightness mode.

This function requires the "Modify system settings" permission. If the permission is not granted, a SecurityException will be thrown and the user will be redirected to the permission settings screen.

methods.name: getMusicVolume
methods.desc: Returns the current media volume.

methods.name: getNotificationVolume
methods.desc: Returns the current notification volume.

methods.name: getAlarmVolume
methods.desc: Returns the current alarm volume.

methods.name: getMusicMaxVolume
methods.desc: Returns the maximum media volume.

methods.name: getNotificationMaxVolume
methods.desc: Returns the maximum notification volume.

methods.name: getAlarmMaxVolume
methods.desc: Returns the maximum alarm volume.

methods.name: setMusicVolume
methods.desc:
Sets the current media volume.

This function requires the "Modify system settings" permission. If the permission is not granted, a SecurityException will be thrown and the user will be redirected to the permission settings screen.

methods.name: setNotificationVolume
methods.desc:
Sets the current notification volume.

This function requires the "Modify system settings" permission. If the permission is not granted, a SecurityException will be thrown and the user will be redirected to the permission settings screen.

methods.name: setAlarmVolume
methods.desc:
Sets the current alarm volume.

This function requires the "Modify system settings" permission. If the permission is not granted, a SecurityException will be thrown and the user will be redirected to the permission settings screen.

methods.name: getBattery
methods.desc: Returns the current battery percentage.

methods.name: isCharging
methods.desc: Returns whether the device is currently charging.

methods.name: getTotalMem
methods.desc: Returns the total device memory in bytes (B). 1MB = 1024 * 1024B.

methods.name: getAvailMem
methods.desc: Returns the currently available memory in bytes (B).

methods.name: isScreenOn
methods.desc:
Returns whether the device screen is on. Returns true if the screen is on; otherwise returns false.

Note: On devices like the vivo Xplay series, the always-on clock when the screen is off does not count as "screen on". Although the screen is technically on, it only displays the clock and is not interactive. In this case, isScreenOn() will also return false.

methods.name: wakeUp
methods.desc: Wakes up the device, including waking up the CPU, screen, etc. Can be used to turn on the screen.

methods.name: wakeUpIfNeeded
methods.desc: Wakes up the device if the screen is not on.

methods.name: keepScreenOn

params.textRaw: `timeout` {number} Duration to keep the screen on, in milliseconds. If not specified, the screen stays on indefinitely.
params.name: timeout
params.desc: Duration to keep the screen on, in milliseconds. If not specified, the screen stays on indefinitely.

methods.desc:
Keeps the screen on.

This function cannot prevent the user from turning off the screen using the lock screen button or similar. It only keeps the screen on when there is no user interaction. Additionally, if the screen is off when this function is called, it will wake the screen.

On some devices, without the timeout parameter, the screen may only stay on within the Auto.js interface and will automatically turn off in other interfaces due to the device's power-saving policy. Therefore, it is recommended to use a long duration instead of "keep screen on indefinitely", for example device.keepScreenOn(3600 * 1000).

You can use device.cancelKeepingAwake() to cancel the screen-on state.

// Keep screen on indefinitely
device.keepScreenOn()

methods.name: keepScreenDim

params.textRaw: `timeout` {number} Duration to keep the screen on, in milliseconds. If not specified, the screen stays on indefinitely.
params.name: timeout
params.desc: Duration to keep the screen on, in milliseconds. If not specified, the screen stays on indefinitely.

methods.desc:
Keeps the screen on but allows it to dim to save power. This function can be used for scheduled scripts that need to wake the screen without requiring the user to watch it, allowing the screen to dim to save battery.

This function cannot prevent the user from turning off the screen using the lock screen button. It only keeps the screen on when there is no user interaction. If the screen is off when called, it will wake the screen.

You can use device.cancelKeepingAwake() to cancel the screen-on state.

methods.name: cancelKeepingAwake
methods.desc: Cancels the device's keep-awake state. Used to cancel screen-on states set by functions like device.keepScreenOn() and device.keepScreenDim().

methods.name: vibrate
methods.desc:
Makes the device vibrate for a period of time.

// Vibrate for two seconds
device.vibrate(2000);

methods.name: cancelVibration
methods.desc: If the device is currently vibrating, this cancels the vibration.
